title: CommonSense Marketplace Discovery
description: Single, versioned document an agent (human-assisted or autonomous) fetches from `/.well-known/commonsc.json` to learn how to publish to the CommonSense algorithm marketplace. This is the agent-first entry point — every URL an agent needs to author, validate, publish, and check the status of an algorithm is reachable from here. A change to this contract is a breaking change to every downstream agent; bump `schemaVersion` accordingly.

schemaVersion.description: Discovery contract version. The customer app and the contributor pipeline both refuse unknown majors — older agents fail loud rather than guess.

lastUpdated.description: ISO-8601 date this discovery doc was last regenerated. Agents may compare against the previous fetch to detect contract changes.

status.description: Optional URL of the platform status page.

schemas.description: Canonical URLs for every schema the contributor pipeline cares about. The customer app pins these by $id and refuses bundles whose manifest references a different schema URL.

tiers.description: Sandbox tiers an algorithm can target. Order is informational; `available: true` is the only thing that gates submission today.

fsReadAllowlist.description: Paths the algorithm may read inside its sandbox. Today: `/input` (host-streamed variants).

fsWriteAllowlist.description: Paths the algorithm may write. Today: `/output` (host-owned scratch).

etaIsoMonth.description: Where `available: false`, the rough ETA — e.g. `2027-01` or `2027-Q2`. Helps an agent decide whether to wait or pick a different tier.

base.description: API root. Endpoint `path` values are appended.

endpoints.description: Named endpoints. Agents should look up by name, not by hardcoded path — the path is allowed to move within a major version of this discovery doc.

rateLimits.description: Free-text rate-limit descriptions, keyed by endpoint name. Soft contract — actual limits are enforced server-side and may be tighter for new publishers.

scheme.description: Today, ed25519 publisher keypair. Future schemes (organisation-owned HSM-backed keys) will be added as additional enum values.

registration.description: URL where a new publisher registers — either a web form (human-driven) or the `publisherRegister` endpoint (agent-driven).

tokenForm.description: How a request authenticates once registered. E.g. `JWT signed with publisher private key, 1h TTL, iss=keyId, aud=api.commonsc.io`.

fixtures.description: Synthetic input fixtures an agent can use to test its entrypoint locally before submitting. All fixtures conform to genomic-io.schema.json — they're real-shaped data, never patient data.

machineGuide.description: Distilled markdown for LLM consumption — typically `https://commonsc.io/llms.txt`.

examples.description: Public repository of reference algorithms. The CommonSense first-party algorithms live here; agents can read them as templates.

queue.description: Every submission gets human review before public catalog promotion. The brief §11 makes auto-publish forbidden; this field locks that in machine-readable form.

slaHours.description: Approximate review turnaround in hours. Soft commitment, not enforced.

policy.description: Reviewer rubric — what gets approved, what gets rejected, how to appeal.

path.description: Either a relative path (joined with `api.base`) or an absolute URL (e.g. for the catalog index, which lives on a separate host).

auth.description: Whether the endpoint requires a publisher token.

requestSchemaRef.description: Optional URL of a JSON Schema describing the POST body, for agents that want to validate before sending.

responseSchemaRef.description: Optional URL of a JSON Schema describing the response. The validate endpoint's response is gate-result.schema.json; the publish endpoint's is a submission stub.
